summary: The source relation N < 8k limits every repeated candidate to at most seven characters, while viable candidate counts depend non-monotonically on source order and multiplicities, so the finite search tree cannot support an honest three-tier scaling verdict.

workload_bound.source_constraint: Because k copies of a length-L candidate require kL source characters and N < 8k, every valid candidate satisfies L <= 7.

replacement_checks.evidence: At most seven distinct characters can each occur k times, candidate depth is at most seven, and every generated extension is checked by one O(N) subsequence scan; prefix pruning retains only candidates that can lead to a longer repeated string.

Authored cases cover the empty result, lexicographic ties, repeated single characters, full-pattern repetition, and answers attaining the seven-character maximum; the exact native source also has remote Accepted evidence.
